title: cleanup: remove stale MCP hosted auth docs
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Removes the stale MCP GitHub OAuth/referrer auth document.
- Removes the non-packaged Cloudflare tunnel setup helper for the old hosted MCP path.
- Leaves the current stdio MCP README/source/auth tools unchanged.

## Evidence
- Current MCP package exposes `setApiKey`, `getKeyInfo`, and `clearApiKey`; the deleted doc referenced missing hosted-auth tools/scripts/endpoints.
- `packages/mcp/package.json` publishes only `src/**/*`, `pollinations-mcp.js`, `README.md`, and `LICENSE`, so the deleted script was not shipped.

## Validation
- `rg -n "GITHUB_AUTH|start-auth|start-auth-test|isAuthenticated|getAuthUrl|listReferrers|addReferrer|removeReferrer|verify-referrer|setup-cloudflare-tunnel|cloudflared" packages/mcp .github package.json --glob '!**/node_modules/**'`
- `npm pack --dry-run` in `packages/mcp`
- `git diff --check origin/main`

gist.headline: Spring Cleaning the MCP Docs
gist.blurb: Swept away some stale documentation and unused scripts for the Model Context Protocol. Keeping the hive clean and tidy!
gist.summary: Removed outdated documentation and unused Cloudflare tunnel scripts related to legacy hosted MCP authentication.
gist.impact: Developers exploring the MCP package will no longer be confused by stale references to unsupported GitHub OAuth and referrer auth flows.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee holding a feather duster, sweeping away old dusty papers (documents) and a broken tunnel pipe (Cloudflare tunnel). Nomnom creature eating the old papers.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding a tiny feather duster, sweeping away dusty old papers. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats the discarded documents. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
